title: CRIU | Feature enhancement
body:
Need features like determining dumping size, dumping time, restoring size, restoring time using podman.

Like CRIU has some command to collect these stats.

comments.body:
@Avijit009

Dumping time:

```shell
# podman container checkpoint -l -k
# crit show /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/cefb2d5b1c96a2899c2457711e29686cebcf1f112b44191c86f08263e0b901b1/userdata/stats-dump 
{
    "magic": "STATS",
    "entries": [
        {
            "dump": {
                "freezing_time": 311,
                "frozen_time": 39239,
                "memdump_time": 549,
                "memwrite_time": 63,
                "pages_scanned": 397,
                "pages_skipped_parent": 0,
                "pages_written": 14,
                "irmap_resolve": 0,
                "pages_lazy": 0,
                "page_pipes": 5,
                "page_pipe_bufs": 5,
                "shpages_scanned": 0,
                "shpages_skipped_parent": 0,
                "shpages_written": 0
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

Dumping size:
```shell
# podman container checkpoint -l -e /tmp/cp.tar
# tar xf dump.tar
# du -hs checkpoint/ rootfs-diff.tar 
130M	checkpoint/
44K	rootfs-diff.tar
```

Restore time, like dump time.

Restore size does not make sense to me.

So all the information can be retrieved. It can be exposed in Podman via something like `# podman container checkpoint -l --stats` if we want. `stats-dump` is a protobuf file and via go-criu we already have the necessary Golang interfaces imported in Podman. This would only be about the CRIU statistics. We could also additionally measure the time Podman needs.

Not sure if this is really useful besides for @Avijit009 and his request as described in https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/criu/issues/1618

Thank you very much. I will follow this procedure.
Maybe this will help me a lot.

Restoring size may not be necessary, but Restoring time is required to know. Right?
So in the case of Restore time determining what should be followed?

> Thank you very much. I will follow this procedure. Maybe this will help me a lot.
> 
> Restoring size may not be necessary, but Restoring time is required to know. Right? So in the case of Restore time determining what should be followed?

As mentioned. Just like dump time but with a different file name. Just have a look at the same location.

Please close this issue if that is all you need to know.

Ok, Thank you.

I guess I got my answers. 

I really appreciate your help. You guys are helping us constantly.
@adrianreber is this worth making descovable via a Podman command?  Or should we just document this in the podman man pages?

> @adrianreber is this worth making descovable via a Podman command? Or should we just document this in the podman man pages?

I am undecided. It sounds like a good idea to have, on the other hand I am not sure how many people would benefit from it.

There is already https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/checkpointctl which gives an overview:
```
checkpointctl show -t /tmp/cp.tar 

Displaying container checkpoint data from /tmp/cp.tar

+-----------------+------------------------------------------+--------------+---------+----------------------+--------+-------------+-------------------+------------+-------------------+
|    CONTAINER    |                  IMAGE                   |      ID      | RUNTIME |       CREATED        | ENGINE |     IP      |        MAC        | CHKPT SIZE | ROOT FS DIFF SIZE |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------+--------------+---------+----------------------+--------+-------------+-------------------+------------+-------------------+
| gifted_mahavira | quay.io/adrianreber/wildfly-hello:latest | 1f3face90bec | crun    | 2021-10-27T06:35:26Z | Podman | 10.88.92.24 | 9a:b0:9e:20:35:37 | 129.5 MiB  | 43.0 KiB          |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------+--------------+---------+----------------------+--------+-------------+-------------------+------------+-------------------+
```

I could extend checkpointctl do also print out timing related informations. Maybe that is enough.

Sorry for reopening this issue again. 

I did not get one point.

To determine the Dumping size:

`# podman container checkpoint -l -e /tmp/cp.tar `  checkpoint the container and save it. 

`# tar xf dump.tar`
`# du -hs checkpoint/ rootfs-diff.tar `
But I did not get the last two commands. How can I find the dump.tar file and checkpoint/ rootfs-diff.tar to get the dump size? 

and every time (dump and restore time) will be in Millisecond?
